title: Politicians and presidential candidates remain in denial about the challenges of adapting to global warming
subhead: As a severe heat wave is disrupting life in France, adapting to climate change remains a blind spot ahead of the 2027 presidential election.

title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer announces resignation
Branko Milanovic, economist: 'There will be no return to neoliberal ideology'
Ten years after Brexit, Britons want closer ties to the EU
subhead: According to a survey by the European Council on Foreign Relations (ECFR) think tank published on Sunday, British voters, disappointed by the 'negative impact' of leaving the EU, stand ready to accept the restoration of freedom of movement between the UK and Europe.

title: Trump-backed candidate wins razor-tight Colombia presidential election
subhead: Abelardo de la Espriella, a 47-year-old lawyer who has never held public office, narrowly won Colombia's presidential runoff. De la Espriella, who had received Donald Trump's 'complete and total endorsement,' promises a return to power for Colombia's right-wing faction.

title: How Ukraine is orchestrating a logistical squeeze on Russian-occupied Crimea
subhead: Using a new generation of drones, Kyiv is waging a methodical campaign targeting the road, rail and energy infrastructure of the peninsula, which Moscow annexed in 2014. Russia is scrambling to adapt its defenses.

title: France celebrates the Fête de la Musique despite heatwave: 'It's just as hot in the apartment where I live anyway'
subhead: Despite some cancellations and with a large portion of the country under a red extreme heat alert, locals and tourists came out for the nationwide music festival that takes place annually on the solstice.

'Twisted Face,' the French Gestapo collaborator who arrested historian and resistance fighter Marc Bloch
subhead: France André, known as 'Twisted Face,' was a staunch French collaborator who led a gang of nearly 100 people in the Lyon area. They spread terror through thefts, arrests, torture and murders, all committed for personal gain and to serve the Nazis. Among his many victims was Marc Bloch, a Jewish historian and resistance fighter who will be inducted into the Panthéon on June 23.

title: What is included in France's comprehensive law against gender-based and sexual violence?
subhead: Following the death of 11-year-old Lyhanna in early June, momentum is growing for a comprehensive law to protect women and children, combining prevention and prosecution measures.

title: The power of music: How we became the conductors of the soundtracks of our lives
subhead: Although listening to music is a more widespread cultural practice than reading books or playing video games, it is less often a subject of debate. Yet the way we listen has evolved with technology, gradually shifting from a collective practice to an individual one, akin to a form of self-conditioning.
